author: The Incarnation of the Word
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.

On the other hand there was the consistency of God's nature, not to be sacrificed for our profit. Were men, then, to be called upon to repent? But repentance cannot avert the execution of a law; still less can it remedy a fallen nature. We have incurred corruption and need to be restored to the Grace of God's Image. None could renew but He Who had created. He alone could (1) recreate all, (2) suffer for all, (3) represent all to the Father.

ccel.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

None could renew but He Who had created. He alone could (1) recreate all, (2) suffer for all, (3) represent all to the Father.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: On the other hand there was the consistency of God’s nature, not to be sacrificed for our profit.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Were men, then, to be called upon to repent?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But repentance cannot avert the execution of a law; still less can it remedy a fallen nature.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: We have incurred corruption and need to be restored to the Grace of God’s Image.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: None could renew but He Who had created.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: He alone could (1) recreate all, (2) suffer for all, (3) represent all to the Father.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But just as this consequence must needs hold, so, too, on the other side the just claims of God lie against it: that God should appear true to the law He had laid down concerning death.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For it were monstrous for God, the Father of truth, to appear a liar for our profit and preservation.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: So here, once more, what possible course was God to take?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: To demand repentance of men for their transgression?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For this one might pronounce worthy of God; as though, just as from transgression men have become set towards corruption, so from repentance they may once more be set in the way of incorruption.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But repentance would, firstly, fail to guard the just claim of God.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For He would still be none the more true, if men did not remain in the grasp of death; nor, secondly, does repentance call men back from what is their nature—it merely stays them from acts of sin.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Now, if there were merely a misdemeanour in question, and not a consequent corruption, repentance were well enough.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if, when transgression had once gained a start, men became involved in that corruption which was their nature, and were deprived of the grace which they had, being in the image of God, what further step was needed? or what was required for such grace and such recall, but the Word of God, which had also at the beginning made everything out of nought?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For His it was once more both to bring the corruptible to incorruption, and to maintain intact the just claim of the Father upon all.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For being Word of the Father, and above all, He alone of natural fitness was both able to recreate everything, and worthy to suffer on behalf of all and to be ambassador for all with the Father.
